channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris speaks about creating economic opportunity for Latino men; Trump airs familiar grievances
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Democratic nominee Kamala Harris on Tuesday said she would work to bring more funds to community banks to help Latino men secure small business loans, while Republican Donald Trump's economic roundtable aimed at Latino voters devolved into a tirade of insults against his opponent.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, JILL COLVIN and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia is behind viral disinformation targeting Walz, intelligence official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Groups in Russia created and helped spread viral disinformation targeting Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz, a senior U.S. intelligence official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris will campaign in Texas to highlight state's abortion ban in a pitch to battleground voters
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris will head to reliably Republican Texas just 10 days before Election Day in an effort to refocus her campaign against former President Donald Trump on reproductive care, which Democrats see as a make-or-break issue this year.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis campaigns against high-stakes abortion measure on Florida ballot: ‘Bait and switch' legislation
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis urged voters in the Sunshine State to vote "No" on the abortion amendment, saying that it would interfere in parental rights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jill Douglas cites education and crime as key issues in Nevada State Senate race
item.description: Jill Douglas, a Republican running for State Senate District 6, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform as she challenges for the seat.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Crypto's impact on 2024 election will be 'fascinating to see': Mulvaney
item.description: NewsNation political contributor Mick Mulvaney said crypto donations in upcoming elections will be "fascinating.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Russia and Iran may fuel violent post-election protests in the US, intelligence officials warn
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russia and Iran may try to encourage violent protests in the U.S. after next month's election, senior intelligence officials warned Tuesday in a declassified memo, citing two recent examples of foreign intelligence agencies seeking to sow discord ahead of the vote.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris says she's ready if Trump tries to prematurely declare victory, isn't worried about sexism
item.description: Kamala Harris says her team is prepared to challenge Donald Trump if he tries to prematurely declare victory in the 2024 election — but first she’s focused beating the Republican nominee.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Georgia Supreme Court rejects Republican attempt to quickly reinstate invalidated election rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Georgia Supreme Court on Tuesday rejected an attempt by national and state Republicans to immediately reinstate recently passed election rules that a judge had ruled were invalid.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security funds under Trump could run out in 6 years: Analysis
item.description: In an analysis published Monday, the fiscal watchdog weighed the impact several Trump proposals — including his plan to end taxation of Social Security benefits
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: More Republicans are voting early, helping break records. Nearly 19 million ballots cast so far
item.description: With former President Donald Trump’s encouragement, Republicans are voting early again, flocking to the polls for in-person voting ahead of Election Day and helping push the national number to nearly 19 million.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The 1.6M voters who could determine the US election don’t currently reside in the country
item.description: An estimated 1.6 million U.S. voters living overseas are eligible to vote in one of seven swing states – which could swing the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump denigrates Harris as 'lazy,' invoking a racist trope against Black people
item.description: Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump criticized Democratic nominee Kamala Harris with a word long used to demean Black people.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Proposition 6 aims to ban forced prison labor in California
item.description: This November, California voters will have the chance to vote on whether to ban involuntary servitude in the state. Proposition 6, if passed by voters, would amend the state constitution to prohibit all forms of involuntary servitude, including forced prison labor.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Harris campaigns make Pennsylvania most expensive battleground, spending $538M combined: report
item.description: More than $538 million in advertising dollars has reportedly been spent in Pennsylvania supporting the presidential runs of Donald Trump and Kamala Harris.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yellen says isolationism 'made America and the world worse off' in speech to global finance leaders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen told world financial leaders Tuesday that the U.S. economy has grown stronger because the Biden administration rejected isolationism, offering a barely veiled criticism of former President Donald Trump’s policies two weeks before the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Project 2025's rightward vision became a flashpoint in this year's election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For the past year, Project 2025 has endured as a persistent force in the presidential election, its far-right proposals deployed by Democrats as shorthand for what Donald Trump would potentially do with a second term at the White House.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris bets her policies can attract Latino voters while Trump touts his time as president to them
item.description: Both Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are highlighting their economic policies in their outreach to Latino voters with Election Day just two weeks away.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Justice Sotomayor says civics education key to future of democracy
item.description: During a visit to Minnesota on Monday, U.S. Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor said that civics education is key to preserving democracy and the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Central Park Five sue Donald Trump for jogger case remarks made at presidential debate
item.description: The men formerly known as the Central Park Five before they were exonerated are suing Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Legality of Musk's $1M offer ‘questionable’: Fmr. Pennsylvania AG
item.description: Bruce Castor, Pennsylvania's former attorney general, said the Tesla billionaire is toeing the line of legality.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Truth vs Deception: DeSantis, advocates clash over Amendment 4
item.description:
The language being used in pro-amendment 4 ads is creating just as much controversy as the amendment itself.
As early voting kicks off Monday, Governor DeSantis stood alongside Florida doctors to speak on voting no on amendment 4, while advocates for voting yes, challenged the argument.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's says it doesn't endorse political candidates after Trump visit
item.description: McDonald's also confirmed that Trump's request to visit the Pennsylvania location was handled at the franchise level.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of migrants heading for southern border
item.description: The hope for many is to cross the border before Donald Trump could be elected back to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats urge Biden to push Israel for press freedom in Gaza
item.description: Dozens of House Democrats are pressing the Biden administration to get tougher on Israel when it comes to press freedoms in Gaza, warning that a lack of media access in the war-torn territory has stifled any push for accountability surrounding military operations that have killed tens of thousands o
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: A frustrated Supreme Court to look at one version of judge shopping
item.description: Both liberal and conservative lawyers have judge-shopped, but in recent years, some conservative-leaning groups have been laser focused on bringing their challenges in the Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Over 1 million voters cast early ballots in North Carolina
item.description: Nearly 1.01 million North Carolinians have already voted in the November general election, state officials reported on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, Fox 5

item.title: Battleground Florida: FEMA funds
item.description: Tampa Bay is working to recover from two recent back-to-back hurricanes that impacted the state weeks away from the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's restless week on the trail ends with a rally riff about Arnold Palmer's manhood
item.description: Former President Donald Trump circuitously referenced golfer Arnold Palmer's genitalia but more directly insulted Vice President Harris at a winding rally in Latrobe, Pa., Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Early voting kicks off in Las Vegas
item.description: As early voting begins in Southern Nevada, Las Vegans are considering who will get their vote ahead of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Tisminezky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris: Trump 'has no idea' about women's health care
item.description: “The man calls himself the father of IVF? I mean what does that even mean?” said Harris, referencing her rival’s comments on in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
